meaning: n.长牙,獠牙,尖牙vt.以牙刺戳,以牙掘,用牙碰伤
examples: The second blind felt the elephant's tusk.
Elephants wear the tusk down faster than they can grow it.
One of the elephants which we saw at the zoo had only one tusk.
Prime Minister Donald Tusk's government signed the Anti-Counterfeiting Trade Agreement in Tokyo on Thursday.
Mr Tusk could easily dump them and sign up the radical anti-clerical Palikot Movement instead.
